title: Hold
description: A patron's hold / reservation on a catalog item (a BookHold) — placed against a CatalogItem and delivered to a pickup Branch. This is the net-new WRITE surface for the domain. NYPL already runs the pieces internally (on-site-hold-request-service, nypl-hold-request-consumer, patron-eligibility-service, ruby-sierra-api-client) and lets patrons place holds inside their authenticated account / the Research Catalog UI, but there is NO clean, documented, agent-native public hold API. This schema serves both the read surface (a patron's own holds) and the HoldRequest write surface (place a hold). Personal patron data is never exposed publicly — reads and writes require the authenticated patron.

holdId.description: Server-assigned hold id (Sierra hold number). Omit on create; returned on the created resource.

status.description: Hold lifecycle status. Set by NYPL, not the patron.

bibId.description: The CatalogItem (bib) being placed on hold.

itemId.description: Specific copy (Sierra item id), when placing an item-level hold.

title.description: Denormalized title of the held item (returned on read).

pickupBranch.description: The Branch where the patron will collect the item (Locations API slug).

requestType.description: hold = borrow when available; recall = request from another branch/ReCAP; edd = electronic document delivery (scan) for research items.

notifyBy.description: How the patron wants to be notified when the hold is ready.
